commands.review_summary:
The PR defers Fireworks manifest model IDs to core’s bundled static catalog, preserves plugin synthesis for non-catalog IDs, explicitly marks two Kimi rows as non-reasoning, and adds catalog-resolution and config-precedence coverage.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +71. Total +72 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main’s Fireworks dynamic resolver clones the default model template for a catalog ID absent from the runtime registry, which can replace that row’s distinct context limits and nested compatibility metadata.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The implementation and proof are ready, but accepting a new canonical provider-resolution owner and coordinating the overlapping companion PR require maintainer judgment rather than automated repair.

commands.author: obuchowski

target.title: fix(fireworks): resolve catalog model params from plugin.json via core
target.body: ## Summary Bundled Fireworks catalog models were being synthesized from the provider default template before core could resolve their per-model metadata from `openclaw.plugin.json`. A fresh `accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6` resolution therefore used a 256,000-token context window instead of the manifest's 262,144-token value. This change: - defers manifest-declared Fireworks model IDs to core's bundled static-catalog resolver; - keeps the plugin dynamic fallback for genuinely uncataloged Fireworks IDs; - marks both bundled Kimi rows as `reasoning: false`, matching their existing runtime behavior; - deep-clones catalog entries so nested compatibility metadata cannot leak through mutation. No Anthropic or OpenAI paths, gateway protocol, config keys, or environment variables change. ## Related work #92217 is complementary and broader: it adds more Fireworks reasoning models and family-specific thinking compatibility. It overlaps this PR in the Fireworks resolver/catalog files and should rebase after this focused fix lands. ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: bundled Fireworks Kimi models now resolve their per-model manifest limits and compatibility metadata instead of inheriting the provider default template. Real environment tested: contributor Oracle Linux aarch64 checkout and maintainer macOS checkout, both using a real Fireworks API key. Exact steps or command run after this patch: fresh-state `openclaw capability model inspect --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6 --json`; embedded local agent run on the same model with prompt `READY`; contributor `model.run` calls with thinking off and high. Evidence after fix: copied live output reported `finalAssistantVisibleText: "READY"`, `winnerProvider: "fireworks"`, `winnerModel: "accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6"`, `fallbackUsed: false`, `contextWindow: 262144`, and `reasoning: false`; terminal screenshots: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/464c47f8-4a60-4018-8068-e777dfe4ee0c and https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a4e4281d-c8f2-421a-a81e-2df5fba936f8. Observed result after fix: the manifest-declared Kimi K2.6 context limit is authoritative while Kimi K2.5 Turbo retains its nested unsupported-tool-schema compatibility metadata. What was not tested: live calls for unrelated Fireworks model families and all non-Fireworks providers; those runtime paths are unchanged. ### Supporting detail Contributor proof used a real Fireworks API key on Oracle Linux: - `openclaw capability model inspect` reported `kimi-k2p6` with `contextWindow: 262144` and `reasoning: false`; - live `model.run` calls with thinking off/high returned the requested output without visible chain-of-thought leakage; - `kimi-k2p5-turbo` retained its nested `compat.unsupportedToolSchemaKeywords` metadata. Maintainer proof on final head `22fdc39adb6dc5032bc5fa6c1f577ceed15d26a5` used a fresh temporary state and a real Fireworks key: - an embedded agent run on `fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/kimi-k2p6` returned exactly `READY`; - the run selected Fireworks/Kimi K2.6 directly with no fallback; - same-state model inspection reported `contextWindow: 262144`, `reasoning: false`, and text/image input support. On current `main`, the equivalent direct resolver probe returned `contextWindow: 256000`, reproducing the bug. ## Verification - `pnpm test extensions/fireworks src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts` - embedded runner: 117 passed - Fireworks plugin: 14 passed - `pnpm format:check -- ` - `pnpm lint:extensions` - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` - `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - `pnpm build` - `git diff --check` - fresh Codex autoreview, branch mode: no accepted/actionable findings The pushed branch was kept merge-based because the contributor fork rejected a rebased push containing upstream workflow history. GitHub's merge tree exactly matches the reviewed and tested rebased tree: `9091ad660633ac9d34a8d4c58b639278e52c894c`. ## Risk The behavior change is limited to Fireworks manifest IDs. User-configured model entries still take precedence, and uncataloged IDs still use the existing dynamic resolver. Regression tests cover both manifest handoff and user-config precedence. ## Current state Final head: `22fdc39adb6dc5032bc5fa6c1f577ceed15d26a5`. Implementation review, focused tests, type checks, lint/format, build, and live Fireworks E2E are complete. Remaining gate: required CI on the final SHA, then squash merge.
